mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects rising aggregate labor income that boosts corporate revenues and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of PAYEMS growth on 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03, or positive returns do not exceed baseline frequency (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor demand and falling household income, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 3.6% as of Nov 2024
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate combined with decelerating consumer prices indicates restrictive real monetary policy that raises corporate borrowing costs and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 2.39% and CPI at 255.155 as of latest prints
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to significantly predict negative returns (t-stat < -1.5) over a 5-year rolling window, or the effect is explained by single variables alone.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a decline in industrial production growth signals weakening capacity utilization and incoming corporate earnings compression, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production at 109.56 as of Nov 2024
failure_condition: In identified late-cycle periods, INDPRO slowdowns do not precede negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC is not negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in retail sales indicate a contraction in consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at 519020m as of Nov 2024
failure_condition: Triggered instances fail to produce negative 5-day returns in at least 55% of cases over any 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, sustained restrictive financing conditions elevate discount rates and debt service burdens, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Episodes with FEDFUNDS above 5% do not produce significantly negative mean 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.10) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and falling retail sales generate a reinforcing contraction in household income and consumption, which amplifies equity downside risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of UNRATE and RSAFS is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in predicting negative returns, or either variable alone explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 5% year-over-year triggers negative earnings revisions via input cost compression and anticipated aggressive monetary response, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL y/y change exceeds 5%, positive 5-day returns occur in more than 45% of instances, rejecting the systematic negative directional prediction.
